answer:
Discord API authentication is performed using the Authorization HTTP header in the format Authorization: TOKEN_TYPE TOKEN [1][2]. There are two primary methods:

For server-to-server communication, use a bot token [3]. You must prefix the token with Bot [3][2].
Authorization: Bot YOUR_BOT_TOKEN [3]

For authenticated user actions, use an OAuth2 bearer token obtained through the OAuth2 API [1][2].
Authorization: Bearer USER_ACCESS_TOKEN [3]

When using OAuth2, you typically exchange an authorization code for an access token via the /api/oauth2/token endpoint [4][3]. This request requires either HTTP Basic authentication (using client_id and client_secret) or the inclusion of these credentials in the form data body [4][5].
